props.text: MiroShark simulations now produce a machine-readable trading signal. GET /api/simulation/<id>/signal.json returns a single JSON line with direction (Bullish / Neutral / Bearish), confidence_pct (0 = pure three-way split, 100 = unanimous), risk_tier (low / medium / high, mapped from quality health), and the three component percentages. A quant tool, Zapier / Make / n8n workflow, or alert pipeline can branch on direction without parsing CSV or reading a chart.

Five export surfaces already exist (trajectory.csv, chart.svg, transcript, reproduce.json, notebook.ipynb) — all of them describe what happened. None of them produces a signal. The audience holding $MIROSHARK has shifted from "people who run sims" to "people who use sim output in trading workflows" — btcbabycow CN tweet "米罗莎要来了", first JP coverage @m000_crypto, and the 5-session ATH streak driving FDV from $1.18M → $3.09M all converge on the same gap. PR #91 closes the last mile between "a sim produces data" and "a sim produces a signal."

backend/app/services/signal_service.py (new, ~210 LoC stdlib): compute_signal() reads the same belief.final percentages the embed-summary builds, picks the plurality stance with a documented tie-break order (bullish > bearish > neutral), computes confidence as distance from the 33.3% noise floor, maps quality health → risk tier with a default-to-high posture for unknown quality.

backend/app/api/simulation.py: new GET /<sim_id>/signal.json route — publish-gated, 404 on sims without a final block, pretty-printed sorted-keys output, 5-minute cache, increments the new signal_json surface counter.
backend/openapi.yaml: TradingSignal schema + operation under Publish & Embed; signal_json registered on SimulationSurfaceStats so OpenAPI consumers see the counter.

backend/tests/test_unit_signal_service.py (new, 26 offline tests): payload shape, plurality + tie-break edges (3-way split, 50/50 bullish/bearish, bearish==neutral), confidence anchors (33.3⇒0, 100⇒100, 66.7⇒~50), every None-returning input path, risk-tier mapping across all health values, ISO-8601 UTC + Z shape.

frontend/src/components/EmbedDialog.vue: 📡 Trading signal section beneath the chart row — direction/confidence badge in header, preview rows for direction + confidence + risk tier + breakdown, Download .json + Copy URL + Copy curl, loads on dialog open and re-fetches when publish flips.

Pure derivation. No new computation. The underlying numbers are the same ones the gallery card already displays, the share card PNG already renders, and the trajectory CSV already serves — a "Bullish 62%" signal here matches every other surface byte-for-byte; only the shape is new. Confidence formula is (leading_pct - 33.333) / 66.667 * 100 clamped to [0, 100] and rounded to 1 dp, so a quant consumer can interpret the field on paper without reading docs. signal_generated_at re-derives on every request (it tracks the signal computation time, not the underlying sim) — bytewise determinism is deliberately not a property of this surface, unlike reproduce.json / notebook.ipynb whose bytes need to be citation-hashable.

A ?format=text variant for cron / email consumers, a signal-history.csv historical series (one row per round) for signal-stability research, and a "consensus alert" webhook variant that posts on every direction flip during a live run.
